title: Cindermark Storks
content:
Cillian Quillen (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as head coach of the Cindermark Storks when they won the Northern Conference title in 1921.

Legacy.

Historians of the Cindermark Storks cite Cillian Quillen's 1921 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Northaven Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.

Awards and honors.

Following the Northern Conference title, Cillian Quillen received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Championship season.

The 1921 Northern Conference title campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Cillian Quillen averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Northaven Memorial Arena in Northaven.

Personal life.

Cillian Quillen retired to Northaven and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1923.
